title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to `target`. You may assume that each input has exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice. Return the answer as a list of two indices.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string `s` containing only the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. A string is valid if: open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets, and open brackets are closed in the correct order. Every close bracket has a corresponding open bracket of the same type.

title: Reverse a Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the reversed list. You can define your own linked list node structure or assume a standard one with `val` and `next` attributes.

title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
description: Given two sorted integer arrays `nums1` and `nums2`, merge them into a single sorted array. Return the merged sorted array.
constraints: Both arrays are sorted in non-decreasing order

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings `strs`, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An anagram is a word formed by rearranging the letters of another word, using all original letters exactly once.
constraints: strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters

title: LRU Cache
description: Design and implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache. It should support two operations: `get(key)` — returns the value if the key exists, otherwise -1, and `put(key, value)` — inserts or updates the value. When the cache reaches its capacity, it should evict the least recently used item before inserting a new one. Both operations should run in O(1) time.

examples.explanation: Key 2 was evicted because it was least recently used when key 3 was inserted

constraints: At most 2 * 10^5 calls to get and put

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values (i.e., from left to right, level by level). You can define your own tree node structure or assume a standard one with `val`, `left`, and `right` attributes.

examples.explanation: Each level of the tree becomes a subarray

title: Top K Frequent Words
description: Given a list of strings `words` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent words. The answer should be sorted by frequency from highest to lowest. If two words have the same frequency, sort them alphabetically.

examples.explanation: 'is' appears 3 times, 'the' appears 3 times — 'is' comes first alphabetically
'i' and 'love' appear twice, 'coding' and 'python' once — 'coding' < 'python' alphabetically

constraints: words[i] consists of lowercase English letters

title: Spiral Matrix
description: Given an m x n matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order (clockwise from the top-left corner).

examples.explanation: Traverse clockwise: top row, right column, bottom row reversed, left column upward, then inward

title: API Rate Limiter
description: Design a rate limiter class that limits the number of requests a user can make within a sliding time window. Implement a `RateLimiter` class with: `__init__(self, max_requests, window_seconds)` — initializes the limiter with a max number of requests allowed within the given time window, and `is_allowed(self, user_id, timestamp)` — returns True if the request is allowed, False if the user has exceeded their limit.

examples.input: limiter = RateLimiter(3, 60); limiter.is_allowed('user1', 1) -> True; limiter.is_allowed('user1', 2) -> True; limiter.is_allowed('user1', 3) -> True; limiter.is_allowed('user1', 4) -> False
examples.explanation: User1 made 3 requests within 60 seconds, 4th request is blocked

constraints: Timestamps are in seconds and monotonically increasing

title: Flatten Nested JSON
description: Write a function that takes a nested dictionary (JSON object) and returns a flattened dictionary where nested keys are joined by dots. Arrays should use numeric indices as keys.
constraints: Values are strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, or objects

title: Shortest Path in Weighted Graph
description: Given `n` nodes labeled from 0 to n-1 and a list of weighted edges `edges` where each edge is [from, to, weight], find the shortest path from node `source` to node `destination`. Return the shortest distance. If no path exists, return -1. The graph is directed.

title: Task Scheduler
description: Given a list of tasks represented by characters and a non-negative integer `n` representing the cooldown interval between two same tasks, return the minimum number of intervals the CPU will take to finish all tasks. During the cooldown interval, the CPU can execute different tasks or remain idle.

examples.explanation: A -> B -> idle -> A -> B -> idle -> A -> B
No cooldown needed, execute all 6 tasks

title: Data Pipeline Processor
description: Design a simple data pipeline class that supports chaining transformations. Implement a `Pipeline` class with: `add_step(func)` — adds a transformation function to the pipeline, and `execute(data)` — runs all steps sequentially on the input data, where each step receives the output of the previous step. Also implement `add_filter(predicate)` — adds a filter step that removes items not matching the predicate (assumes data is a list at that point).

examples.input: p = Pipeline(); p.add_step(lambda x: [i*2 for i in x]); p.add_filter(lambda x: x > 4); p.execute([1,2,3,4,5])
examples.explanation: Double all values, then filter those <= 4

constraints: Pipeline should support any number of steps
Each step receives output of previous step

title: Event Emitter
description: Implement an EventEmitter class that supports registering event listeners and emitting events. The class should support: `on(event, callback)` — registers a callback for an event, `off(event, callback)` — removes a specific callback for an event, `emit(event, *args)` — triggers all callbacks registered for the event with the provided arguments, and `once(event, callback)` — registers a callback that fires only once.

examples.explanation: Event is emitted and handler receives the argument

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize that string back to the original tree structure. The serialization format is up to you — just ensure that the tree can be perfectly reconstructed. Define your own tree node structure if needed.

examples.output: serialize(root) produces a string; deserialize(string) reproduces the original tree
examples.explanation: The tree should be identical after a round-trip serialize-deserialize
